description_long: This object is a futuristic, streamlined airplane with a sharply pointed nose and a narrow fuselage that widens toward the rear. The most distinctive feature is its dual circular engine nacelles mounted above the main body, each encircling a glowing blue ring, suggesting advanced propulsion technology. The wings are integrated into the body and are relatively short, with the engine nacelles forming the primary lateral extension. The tail section is compact, with a pair of upward-canted vertical stabilizers and no visible horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with a dark, tinted canopy that blends smoothly into the body. The surface is predominantly a deep blue with lighter blue highlights, giving it a high-tech, aerodynamic appearance.
description: A sleek, blue futuristic airplane with dual circular engine nacelles and a sharply pointed nose, featuring a compact tail and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit, and the two large circular engine nacelles with glowing blue rings.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, the integration of the engine nacelles above the body, and the sharply tapered nose.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin upward-canted vertical stabilizers and the full circular structure of the engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the fuselage profile and the engine nacelles' placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-mounted, integrated wing structure with dual circular nacelles forming the primary lateral extension; no traditional straight or swept wings; upward-canted vertical stabilizers.
attribute_engine_type: Twin circular electric or jet engines mounted above the fuselage within nacelles.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers in an upward-canted configuration; no visible horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a dark, streamlined canopy integrated into the nose section.
